""" version info, help messages, tracing configuration.  """
import os
import sys
from argparse import Action

import py

import pytest
from _pytest.config import PrintHelp


class HelpAction(Action):
    """This is an argparse Action that will raise an exception in
    order to skip the rest of the argument parsing when --help is passed.
    This prevents argparse from quitting due to missing required arguments
    when any are defined, for example by ``pytest_addoption``.
    This is similar to the way that the builtin argparse --help option is
    implemented by raising SystemExit.
    """

    def __init__(self, option_strings, dest=None, default=False, help=None):
        super().__init__(
            option_strings=option_strings,
            dest=dest,
            const=True,
            default=default,
            nargs=0,
            help=help,
        )

    def __call__(self, parser, namespace, values, option_string=None):
        setattr(namespace, self.dest, self.const)

        # We should only skip the rest of the parsing after preparse is done
        if getattr(parser._parser, "after_preparse", False):
            raise PrintHelp


def pytest_addoption(parser):
    group = parser.getgroup("debugconfig")
    group.addoption(
        "--version",
        action="store_true",
        help="display pytest lib version and import information.",
    )
    group._addoption(
        "-h",
        "--help",
        action=HelpAction,
        dest="help",
        help="show help message and configuration info",
    )
    group._addoption(
        "-p",
        action="append",
        dest="plugins",
        default=[],
        metavar="name",
        help="early-load given plugin module name or entry point (multi-allowed). "
        "To avoid loading of plugins, use the `no:` prefix, e.g. "
        "`no:doctest`.",
    )
    group.addoption(
        "--traceconfig",
        "--trace-config",
        action="store_true",
        default=False,
        help="trace considerations of conftest.py files.",
    ),
    group.addoption(
        "--debug",
        action="store_true",
        dest="debug",
        default=False,
        help="store internal tracing debug information in 'pytestdebug.log'.",
    )
    group._addoption(
        "-o",
        "--override-ini",
        dest="override_ini",
        action="append",
        help='override ini option with "option=value" style, e.g. `-o xfail_strict=True -o cache_dir=cache`.',
    )


@pytest.hookimpl(hookwrapper=True)
def pytest_cmdline_parse():
    outcome = yield
    config = outcome.get_result()
    if config.option.debug:
        path = os.path.abspath("pytestdebug.log")
        debugfile = open(path, "w")
        debugfile.write(
            "versions pytest-%s, py-%s, "
            "python-%s\ncwd=%s\nargs=%s\n\n"
            % (
                pytest.__version__,
                py.__version__,
                ".".join(map(str, sys.version_info)),
                os.getcwd(),
                config.invocation_params.args,
            )
        )
        config.trace.root.setwriter(debugfile.write)
        undo_tracing = config.pluginmanager.enable_tracing()
        sys.stderr.write("writing pytestdebug information to %s\n" % path)

        def unset_tracing():
            debugfile.close()
            sys.stderr.write("wrote pytestdebug information to %s\n" % debugfile.name)
            config.trace.root.setwriter(None)
            undo_tracing()

        config.add_cleanup(unset_tracing)


def showversion(config):
    p = py.path.local(pytest.__file__)
    sys.stderr.write(
        "This is pytest version {}, imported from {}\n".format(pytest.__version__, p)
    )
    plugininfo = getpluginversioninfo(config)
    if plugininfo:
        for line in plugininfo:
            sys.stderr.write(line + "\n")


def pytest_cmdline_main(config):
    if config.option.version:
        showversion(config)
        return 0
    elif config.option.help:
        config._do_configure()
        showhelp(config)
        config._ensure_unconfigure()
        return 0


def showhelp(config):
    import textwrap

    reporter = config.pluginmanager.get_plugin("terminalreporter")
    tw = reporter._tw
    tw.write(config._parser.optparser.format_help())
    tw.line()
    tw.line(
        "[pytest] ini-options in the first pytest.ini|tox.ini|setup.cfg file found:"
    )
    tw.line()

    columns = tw.fullwidth  # costly call
    indent_len = 24  # based on argparse's max_help_position=24
    indent = " " * indent_len
    for name in config._parser._ininames:
        help, type, default = config._parser._inidict[name]
        if type is None:
            type = "string"
        spec = "{} ({}):".format(name, type)
        tw.write("  %s" % spec)
        spec_len = len(spec)

""" generic mechanism for marking and selecting python functions. """
from .legacy import matchkeyword
from .legacy import matchmark
from .structures import EMPTY_PARAMETERSET_OPTION
from .structures import get_empty_parameterset_mark
from .structures import Mark
from .structures import MARK_GEN
from .structures import MarkDecorator
from .structures import MarkGenerator
from .structures import ParameterSet
from _pytest.config import hookimpl
from _pytest.config import UsageError

__all__ = ["Mark", "MarkDecorator", "MarkGenerator", "get_empty_parameterset_mark"]


def param(*values, **kw):
    """Specify a parameter in `pytest.mark.parametrize`_ calls or
    :ref:`parametrized fixtures <fixture-parametrize-marks>`.

    .. code-block:: python

        @pytest.mark.parametrize("test_input,expected", [
            ("3+5", 8),
            pytest.param("6*9", 42, marks=pytest.mark.xfail),
        ])
        def test_eval(test_input, expected):
            assert eval(test_input) == expected

    :param values: variable args of the values of the parameter set, in order.
    :keyword marks: a single mark or a list of marks to be applied to this parameter set.
    :keyword str id: the id to attribute to this parameter set.
    """
    return ParameterSet.param(*values, **kw)


def pytest_addoption(parser):
    group = parser.getgroup("general")
    group._addoption(
        "-k",
        action="store",
        dest="keyword",
        default="",
        metavar="EXPRESSION",
        help="only run tests which match the given substring expression. "
        "An expression is a python evaluatable expression "
        "where all names are substring-matched against test names "
        "and their parent classes. Example: -k 'test_method or test_"
        "other' matches all test functions and classes whose name "
        "contains 'test_method' or 'test_other', while -k 'not test_method' "
        "matches those that don't contain 'test_method' in their names. "
        "-k 'not test_method and not test_other' will eliminate the matches. "
        "Additionally keywords are matched to classes and functions "
        "containing extra names in their 'extra_keyword_matches' set, "
        "as well as functions which have names assigned directly to them.",
    )

    group._addoption(
        "-m",
        action="store",
        dest="markexpr",
        default="",
        metavar="MARKEXPR",
        help="only run tests matching given mark expression.  "
        "example: -m 'mark1 and not mark2'.",
    )

    group.addoption(
        "--markers",
        action="store_true",
        help="show markers (builtin, plugin and per-project ones).",
    )

    parser.addini("markers", "markers for test functions", "linelist")
    parser.addini(EMPTY_PARAMETERSET_OPTION, "default marker for empty parametersets")


@hookimpl(tryfirst=True)
def pytest_cmdline_main(config):
    import _pytest.config

    if config.option.markers:
        config._do_configure()
        tw = _pytest.config.create_terminal_writer(config)
        for line in config.getini("markers"):
            parts = line.split(":", 1)
            name = parts[0]
            rest = parts[1] if len(parts) == 2 else ""
            tw.write("@pytest.mark.%s:" % name, bold=True)
            tw.line(rest)
            tw.line()
        config._ensure_unconfigure()
        return 0


def deselect_by_keyword(items, config):
    keywordexpr = config.option.keyword.lstrip()
    if not keywordexpr:
        return

    if keywordexpr.startswith("-"):
        keywordexpr = "not " + keywordexpr[1:]
    selectuntil = False
    if keywordexpr[-1:] == ":":
        selectuntil = True
        keywordexpr = keywordexpr[:-1]

    remaining = []
    deselected = []
    for colitem in items:
        if keywordexpr and not matchkeyword(colitem, keywordexpr):
            deselected.append(colitem)
        else:
            if selectuntil:
                keywordexpr = None
            remaining.append(colitem)

    if deselected:
        config.hook.pytest_deselected(items=deselected)
        items[:] = remaining


def deselect_by_mark(items, config):
    matchexpr = config.option.markexpr
    if not matchexpr:
        return

    remaining = []
    deselected = []
    for item in items:
        if matchmark(item, matchexpr):
            remaining.append(item)
        else:
            deselected.append(item)

    if deselected:
        config.hook.pytest_deselected(items=deselected)
        items[:] = remaining


def pytest_collection_modifyitems(items, config):
    deselect_by_keyword(items, config)
    deselect_by_mark(items, config)


def pytest_configure(config):
    config._old_mark_config = MARK_GEN._config
    MARK_GEN._config = config

    empty_parameterset = config.getini(EMPTY_PARAMETERSET_OPTION)

    if empty_parameterset not in ("skip", "xfail", "fail_at_collect", None, ""):
        raise UsageError(
            "{!s} must be one of skip, xfail or fail_at_collect"
            " but it is {!r}".format(EMPTY_PARAMETERSET_OPTION, empty_parameterset)
        )


def pytest_unconfigure(config):
    MARK_GEN._config = getattr(config, "_old_mark_config", None)

        self.excinfo = excinfo  # type: Tuple[Type[Exception], Exception, TracebackType]


def main(args=None, plugins=None) -> "Union[int, _pytest.main.ExitCode]":
    """ return exit code, after performing an in-process test run.

    :arg args: list of command line arguments.

    :arg plugins: list of plugin objects to be auto-registered during
                  initialization.
    """
    from _pytest.main import ExitCode

    try:
        try:
            config = _prepareconfig(args, plugins)
        except ConftestImportFailure as e:
            exc_info = ExceptionInfo(e.excinfo)
            tw = py.io.TerminalWriter(sys.stderr)
            tw.line(
                "ImportError while loading conftest '{e.path}'.".format(e=e), red=True
            )
            exc_info.traceback = exc_info.traceback.filter(filter_traceback)
            exc_repr = (
                exc_info.getrepr(style="short", chain=False)
                if exc_info.traceback
                else exc_info.exconly()
            )
            formatted_tb = str(exc_repr)
            for line in formatted_tb.splitlines():
                tw.line(line.rstrip(), red=True)
            return ExitCode.USAGE_ERROR
        else:
            try:
                ret = config.hook.pytest_cmdline_main(
                    config=config
                )  # type: Union[ExitCode, int]
                try:
                    return ExitCode(ret)
                except ValueError:
                    return ret
            finally:
                config._ensure_unconfigure()
    except UsageError as e:
        tw = py.io.TerminalWriter(sys.stderr)
        for msg in e.args:
            tw.line("ERROR: {}\n".format(msg), red=True)
        return ExitCode.USAGE_ERROR


class cmdline:  # compatibility namespace
    main = staticmethod(main)


def filename_arg(path, optname):
    """ Argparse type validator for filename arguments.

    :path: path of filename
    :optname: name of the option
    """
    if os.path.isdir(path):
        raise UsageError("{} must be a filename, given: {}".format(optname, path))
    return path


def directory_arg(path, optname):
    """Argparse type validator for directory arguments.

    :path: path of directory
    :optname: name of the option
    """
    if not os.path.isdir(path):
        raise UsageError("{} must be a directory, given: {}".format(optname, path))
    return path

def get_config(args=None, plugins=None):
    # subsequent calls to main will create a fresh instance
    pluginmanager = PytestPluginManager()
    config = Config(
        pluginmanager,
        invocation_params=Config.InvocationParams(
            args=args or (), plugins=plugins, dir=Path().resolve()
        ),
    )

    if args is not None:
        # Handle any "-p no:plugin" args.
        pluginmanager.consider_preparse(args)

    for spec in default_plugins:
        pluginmanager.import_plugin(spec)
    return config


def get_plugin_manager():
    """
    Obtain a new instance of the
    :py:class:`_pytest.config.PytestPluginManager`, with default plugins
    already loaded.

    This function can be used by integration with other tools, like hooking
    into pytest to run tests into an IDE.
    """
    return get_config().pluginmanager


def _prepareconfig(args=None, plugins=None):
    if args is None:
        args = sys.argv[1:]
    elif isinstance(args, py.path.local):
        args = [str(args)]
    elif not isinstance(args, (tuple, list)):
        msg = "`args` parameter expected to be a list or tuple of strings, got: {!r} (type: {})"
        raise TypeError(msg.format(args, type(args)))

    config = get_config(args, plugins)
    pluginmanager = config.pluginmanager
    try:
        if plugins:
            for plugin in plugins:
                if isinstance(plugin, str):
                    pluginmanager.consider_pluginarg(plugin)
                else:
                    pluginmanager.register(plugin)
        return pluginmanager.hook.pytest_cmdline_parse(
            pluginmanager=pluginmanager, args=args
        )
    except BaseException:
        config._ensure_unconfigure()
        raise


def _fail_on_non_top_pytest_plugins(conftestpath, confcutdir):
    msg = (
        "Defining 'pytest_plugins' in a non-top-level conftest is no longer supported:\n"
        "It affects the entire test suite instead of just below the conftest as expected.\n"
        "  {}\n"
        "Please move it to a top level conftest file at the rootdir:\n"
        "  {}\n"
        "For more information, visit:\n"
        "  https://docs.pytest.org/en/latest/deprecations.html#pytest-plugins-in-non-top-level-conftest-files"
    )
    fail(msg.format(conftestpath, confcutdir), pytrace=False)


class PytestPluginManager(PluginManager):
    """
    Overwrites :py:class:`pluggy.PluginManager <pluggy.PluginManager>` to add pytest-specific
    functionality:

    * loading plugins from the command line, ``PYTEST_PLUGINS`` env variable and
      ``pytest_plugins`` global variables found in plugins being loaded;
    * ``conftest.py`` loading during start-up;
    """

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__("pytest")
        # The objects are module objects, only used generically.
        self._conftest_plugins = set()  # type: Set[object]

        # state related to local conftest plugins
        # Maps a py.path.local to a list of module objects.
        self._dirpath2confmods = {}  # type: Dict[Any, List[object]]
        # Maps a py.path.local to a module object.
        self._conftestpath2mod = {}  # type: Dict[Any, object]
        self._confcutdir = None
        self._noconftest = False
        # Set of py.path.local's.
        self._duplicatepaths = set()  # type: Set[Any]

        self.add_hookspecs(_pytest.hookspec)
        self.register(self)
        if os.environ.get("PYTEST_DEBUG"):
            err = sys.stderr
            encoding = getattr(err, "encoding", "utf8")
            try:
                err = py.io.dupfile(err, encoding=encoding)
            except Exception:
                pass
            self.trace.root.setwriter(err.write)
            self.enable_tracing()

        # Config._consider_importhook will set a real object if required.
        self.rewrite_hook = _pytest.assertion.DummyRewriteHook()
        # Used to know when we are importing conftests after the pytest_configure stage
        self._configured = False

    def parse_hookimpl_opts(self, plugin, name):
        # pytest hooks are always prefixed with pytest_
        # so we avoid accessing possibly non-readable attributes
        # (see issue #1073)
        if not name.startswith("pytest_"):
            return
        # ignore names which can not be hooks
        if name == "pytest_plugins":
            return

        method = getattr(plugin, name)
        opts = super().parse_hookimpl_opts(plugin, name)

        # consider only actual functions for hooks (#3775)
        if not inspect.isroutine(method):
            return

        # collect unmarked hooks as long as they have the `pytest_' prefix
        if opts is None and name.startswith("pytest_"):
            opts = {}
        if opts is not None:
            # TODO: DeprecationWarning, people should use hookimpl
            # https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/4562
            known_marks = {m.name for m in getattr(method, "pytestmark", [])}

            for name in ("tryfirst", "trylast", "optionalhook", "hookwrapper"):
                opts.setdefault(name, hasattr(method, name) or name in known_marks)
        return opts

    def parse_hookspec_opts(self, module_or_class, name):
        opts = super().parse_hookspec_opts(module_or_class, name)
        if opts is None:
            method = getattr(module_or_class, name)

            if name.startswith("pytest_"):
                # todo: deprecate hookspec hacks
                # https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/4562
                known_marks = {m.name for m in getattr(method, "pytestmark", [])}
                opts = {
                    "firstresult": hasattr(method, "firstresult")
                    or "firstresult" in known_marks,
                    "historic": hasattr(method, "historic")
                    or "historic" in known_marks,
                }
        return opts

    def register(self, plugin, name=None):
        if name in _pytest.deprecated.DEPRECATED_EXTERNAL_PLUGINS:
            warnings.warn(
                PytestConfigWarning(
                    "{} plugin has been merged into the core, "
                    "please remove it from your requirements.".format(
                        name.replace("_", "-")
                    )
                )
            )
            return
        ret = super().register(plugin, name)
        if ret:
            self.hook.pytest_plugin_registered.call_historic(
                kwargs=dict(plugin=plugin, manager=self)
            )

            if isinstance(plugin, types.ModuleType):
                self.consider_module(plugin)
        return ret

    def getplugin(self, name):
        # support deprecated naming because plugins (xdist e.g.) use it
        return self.get_plugin(name)

    def hasplugin(self, name):
        """Return True if the plugin with the given name is registered."""
        return bool(self.get_plugin(name))

    def pytest_configure(self, config):
        # XXX now that the pluginmanager exposes hookimpl(tryfirst...)
        # we should remove tryfirst/trylast as markers
        config.addinivalue_line(
            "markers",
            "tryfirst: mark a hook implementation function such that the "
            "plugin machinery will try to call it first/as early as possible.",
        )
        config.addinivalue_line(
            "markers",
            "trylast: mark a hook implementation function such that the "
            "plugin machinery will try to call it last/as late as possible.",
        )
        self._configured = True

    #
    # internal API for local conftest plugin handling
    #
    def _set_initial_conftests(self, namespace):
        """ load initial conftest files given a preparsed "namespace".
            As conftest files may add their own command line options
            which have arguments ('--my-opt somepath') we might get some
            false positives.  All builtin and 3rd party plugins will have
            been loaded, however, so common options will not confuse our logic
            here.
        """
        current = py.path.local()
        self._confcutdir = (
            current.join(namespace.confcutdir, abs=True)
            if namespace.confcutdir
            else None
        )
        self._noconftest = namespace.noconftest
        self._using_pyargs = namespace.pyargs
        testpaths = namespace.file_or_dir
        foundanchor = False
        for path in testpaths:
            path = str(path)
            # remove node-id syntax
            i = path.find("::")
            if i != -1:
                path = path[:i]
            anchor = current.join(path, abs=1)
            if exists(anchor):  # we found some file object
                self._try_load_conftest(anchor)
                foundanchor = True
        if not foundanchor:
            self._try_load_conftest(current)

    def _try_load_conftest(self, anchor):
        self._getconftestmodules(anchor)
        # let's also consider test* subdirs
        if anchor.check(dir=1):
            for x in anchor.listdir("test*"):
                if x.check(dir=1):
                    self._getconftestmodules(x)

    @lru_cache(maxsize=128)
    def _getconftestmodules(self, path):
        if self._noconftest:
            return []

        if path.isfile():
            directory = path.dirpath()
        else:
            directory = path

        # XXX these days we may rather want to use config.rootdir
        # and allow users to opt into looking into the rootdir parent
        # directories instead of requiring to specify confcutdir
        clist = []
        for parent in directory.realpath().parts():
            if self._confcutdir and self._confcutdir.relto(parent):
                continue
            conftestpath = parent.join("conftest.py")
            if conftestpath.isfile():
                mod = self._importconftest(conftestpath)
                clist.append(mod)
        self._dirpath2confmods[directory] = clist
        return clist

    def _rget_with_confmod(self, name, path):
        modules = self._getconftestmodules(path)
        for mod in reversed(modules):
            try:
                return mod, getattr(mod, name)
            except AttributeError:
                continue
        raise KeyError(name)

    def _importconftest(self, conftestpath):
        # Use a resolved Path object as key to avoid loading the same conftest twice
        # with build systems that create build directories containing
        # symlinks to actual files.
        # Using Path().resolve() is better than py.path.realpath because
        # it resolves to the correct path/drive in case-insensitive file systems (#5792)
        key = Path(str(conftestpath)).resolve()
        try:
            return self._conftestpath2mod[key]
        except KeyError:
            pkgpath = conftestpath.pypkgpath()
            if pkgpath is None:
                _ensure_removed_sysmodule(conftestpath.purebasename)
            try:
                mod = conftestpath.pyimport()
                if (
                    hasattr(mod, "pytest_plugins")
                    and self._configured
                    and not self._using_pyargs
                ):
                    _fail_on_non_top_pytest_plugins(conftestpath, self._confcutdir)
            except Exception:
                raise ConftestImportFailure(conftestpath, sys.exc_info())

            self._conftest_plugins.add(mod)
            self._conftestpath2mod[key] = mod
            dirpath = conftestpath.dirpath()
            if dirpath in self._dirpath2confmods:
                for path, mods in self._dirpath2confmods.items():
                    if path and path.relto(dirpath) or path == dirpath:
                        assert mod not in mods
                        mods.append(mod)
            self.trace("loaded conftestmodule %r" % (mod))
            self.consider_conftest(mod)
            return mod

    #
    # API for bootstrapping plugin loading
    #
    #

    def consider_preparse(self, args):
        i = 0
        n = len(args)
        while i < n:
            opt = args[i]
            i += 1

"""

"""
import collections

from io import StringIO

import pluggy

import pytest

from _pytest.reports import BaseReport
from _pytest.terminal import _folded_skips
from _pytest.terminal import _get_line_with_reprcrash_message

from _pytest.terminal import build_summary_stats_line
from _pytest.terminal import getreportopt

DistInfo = collections.namedtuple("DistInfo", ["project_name", "version"])
RED = r"\x1b\[31m"
GREEN = r"\x1b\[32m"
YELLOW = r"\x1b\[33m"
RESET = r"\x1b\[0m"


class Option:
    def __init__(self, verbosity=0):
        self.verbosity = verbosity

    @property
    def args(self):
        values = []
        values.append("--verbosity=%d" % self.verbosity)
        return values


@pytest.fixture(
    params=[Option(verbosity=0), Option(verbosity=1), Option(verbosity=-1)],
    ids=["default", "verbose", "quiet"],
)
def option(request):
    return request.param


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "input,expected",
    [
        ([DistInfo(project_name="test", version=1)], ["test-1"]),
        ([DistInfo(project_name="pytest-test", version=1)], ["test-1"]),
        (
            [
                DistInfo(project_name="test", version=1),
                DistInfo(project_name="test", version=1),
            ],
            ["test-1"],
        ),
    ],
    ids=["normal", "prefix-strip", "deduplicate"],
)
def test_plugin_nameversion(input, expected):
    pluginlist = [(None, x) for x in input]
    result = _plugin_nameversions(pluginlist)
    assert result == expected


class TestTerminal:
    def test_pass_skip_fail(self, testdir, option):
        testdir.makepyfile(
            """
            import pytest
            def test_ok():
                pass
            def test_skip():
                pytest.skip("xx")
            def test_func():
                assert 0
        """
        )
        result = testdir.runpytest(*option.args)
        if option.verbosity > 0:
            result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(
                [
                    "*test_pass_skip_fail.py::test_ok PASS*",
                    "*test_pass_skip_fail.py::test_skip SKIP*",
                    "*test_pass_skip_fail.py::test_func FAIL*",
                ]
            )
        elif option.verbosity == 0:
            result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(["*test_pass_skip_fail.py .sF*"])
        else:
            result.stdout.fnmatch_lines([".sF*"])
        result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(
            ["    def test_func():", ">       assert 0", "E       assert 0"]
        )

    def test_internalerror(self, testdir, linecomp):
        modcol = testdir.getmodulecol("def test_one(): pass")
        rep = TerminalReporter(modcol.config, file=linecomp.stringio)
        with pytest.raises(ValueError) as excinfo:
            raise ValueError("hello")
        rep.pytest_internalerror(excinfo.getrepr())
        linecomp.assert_contains_lines(["INTERNALERROR> *ValueError*hello*"])

    def test_writeline(self, testdir, linecomp):
        modcol = testdir.getmodulecol("def test_one(): pass")
        rep = TerminalReporter(modcol.config, file=linecomp.stringio)
        rep.write_fspath_result(modcol.nodeid, ".")
        rep.write_line("hello world")
        lines = linecomp.stringio.getvalue().split("\n")
        assert not lines[0]
        assert lines[1].endswith(modcol.name + " .")
        assert lines[2] == "hello world"

    def test_show_runtest_logstart(self, testdir, linecomp):
        item = testdir.getitem("def test_func(): pass")
        tr = TerminalReporter(item.config, file=linecomp.stringio)
        item.config.pluginmanager.register(tr)
        location = item.reportinfo()
        tr.config.hook.pytest_runtest_logstart(
            nodeid=item.nodeid, location=location, fspath=str(item.fspath)
        )
        linecomp.assert_contains_lines(["*test_show_runtest_logstart.py*"])

    def test_runtest_location_shown_before_test_starts(self, testdir):
        testdir.makepyfile(
            """
            def test_1():
                import time
                time.sleep(20)
        """
        )
        child = testdir.spawn_pytest("")
        child.expect(".*test_runtest_location.*py")
        child.sendeof()
        child.kill(15)

    def test_report_collect_after_half_a_second(self, testdir):
        """Test for "collecting" being updated after 0.5s"""

        testdir.makepyfile(
            **{
                "test1.py": """
                import _pytest.terminal

                _pytest.terminal.REPORT_COLLECTING_RESOLUTION = 0

                def test_1():
                    pass
                    """,
                "test2.py": "def test_2(): pass",
            }
        )

        child = testdir.spawn_pytest("-v test1.py test2.py")
        child.expect(r"collecting \.\.\.")
        child.expect(r"collecting 1 item")
        child.expect(r"collecting 2 items")
        child.expect(r"collected 2 items")
        rest = child.read().decode("utf8")
        assert "= \x1b[32m\x1b[1m2 passed\x1b[0m\x1b[32m in" in rest

    def test_itemreport_subclasses_show_subclassed_file(self, testdir):
        testdir.makepyfile(
            test_p1="""
            class BaseTests(object):
                def test_p1(self):
                    pass
            class TestClass(BaseTests):
                pass
        """
        )
        p2 = testdir.makepyfile(
            test_p2="""
            from test_p1 import BaseTests
            class TestMore(BaseTests):
                pass
        """
        )
        result = testdir.runpytest(p2)
        result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(["*test_p2.py .*", "*1 passed*"])
        result = testdir.runpytest("-vv", p2)
        result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(
            ["*test_p2.py::TestMore::test_p1* <- *test_p1.py*PASSED*"]
        )

    def test_itemreport_directclasses_not_shown_as_subclasses(self, testdir):

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("fulltrace", ("", "--fulltrace"))
    def test_keyboard_interrupt(self, testdir, fulltrace):
        testdir.makepyfile(
            """
            def test_foobar():
                assert 0
            def test_spamegg():
                import py; pytest.skip('skip me please!')
            def test_interrupt_me():
                raise KeyboardInterrupt   # simulating the user
        """
        )

        result = testdir.runpytest(fulltrace, no_reraise_ctrlc=True)
        result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(
            [
                "    def test_foobar():",
                ">       assert 0",
                "E       assert 0",
                "*_keyboard_interrupt.py:6: KeyboardInterrupt*",
            ]
        )
        if fulltrace:
            result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(
                ["*raise KeyboardInterrupt   # simulating the user*"]
            )
        else:
            result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(
                ["(to show a full traceback on KeyboardInterrupt use --full-trace)"]
            )
        result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(["*KeyboardInterrupt*"])

    def test_keyboard_in_sessionstart(self, testdir):
        testdir.makeconftest(
            """
            def pytest_sessionstart():
                raise KeyboardInterrupt
        """
        )
        testdir.makepyfile(
            """
            def test_foobar():
                pass
        """
        )

        result = testdir.runpytest(no_reraise_ctrlc=True)
        assert result.ret == 2
        result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(["*KeyboardInterrupt*"])

    def test_collect_single_item(self, testdir):
        """Use singular 'item' when reporting a single test item"""
        testdir.makepyfile(
            """
            def test_foobar():
                pass
        """
        )
        result = testdir.runpytest()
        result.stdout.fnmatch_lines(["collected 1 item"])

    def test_rewrite(self, testdir, monkeypatch):
        config = testdir.parseconfig()
        f = StringIO()
        monkeypatch.setattr(f, "isatty", lambda *args: True)
        tr = TerminalReporter(config, f)
        tr._tw.fullwidth = 10
        tr.write("hello")
        tr.rewrite("hey", erase=True)
        assert f.getvalue() == "hello" + "\r" + "hey" + (6 * " ")

    def inline_runsource(self, source, *cmdlineargs):
        """Run a test module in process using ``pytest.main()``.

        This run writes "source" into a temporary file and runs
        ``pytest.main()`` on it, returning a :py:class:`HookRecorder` instance
        for the result.

        :param source: the source code of the test module

        :param cmdlineargs: any extra command line arguments to use

        :return: :py:class:`HookRecorder` instance of the result

        """
        p = self.makepyfile(source)
        values = list(cmdlineargs) + [p]
        return self.inline_run(*values)

    def inline_genitems(self, *args):
        """Run ``pytest.main(['--collectonly'])`` in-process.

        Runs the :py:func:`pytest.main` function to run all of pytest inside
        the test process itself like :py:meth:`inline_run`, but returns a
        tuple of the collected items and a :py:class:`HookRecorder` instance.

        """
        rec = self.inline_run("--collect-only", *args)
        items = [x.item for x in rec.getcalls("pytest_itemcollected")]

So when shortupper=False (default for addoption), lowercase shortopts are reserved (meaning disallowed). Using _addoption sets shortupper=True, allowing lowercase short options. Checkpoint 4: Propose a fix, then immediately challenge it

Proposed fix (code change)
- Change the option registration for collect-only to include a one-character shortcut.
- Minimal and safe choice: add an uppercase short option "-C" for --collect-only.

Why "-C"?
- Many single-letter short flags are already used in pytest and are lowercase: -x, -c, -s, -h, -p, -o, -k, -m, -v, -q, -r, -l, ...
- Lowercase letters are heavily used; choosing an uppercase letter minimizes risk of colliding with existing conventions.
- Uppercase short flags are allowed by the option registration machinery (OptionGroup.addoption accepts short options where the single-letter is not lowercase; addoption enforces that lowercase shortoptions are reserved).
- I searched for "-C" and found no uses in the repo, so "-C" is available.

Patch (one-line change in src/_pytest/main.py):

Replace the block:
- group.addoption(
-     "--collectonly",
-     "--collect-only",
-     action="store_true",
-     help="only collect tests, don't execute them.",
- ),
with:
+ group._addoption(
+     "-C",
+     "--collectonly",
+     "--collect-only",
+     action="store_true",
+     help="only collect tests, don't execute them.",
+ ),

(Alternatively, group.addoption("-C", "--collectonly", "--collect-only", ...) also works, but using _addoption is consistent with how other single-letter options are registered and doesn't change the resulting dest.)

Concrete patch (unified diff style):
--- a/src/_pytest/main.py
+++ b/src/_pytest/main.py
@@
-    group.addoption(
-        "--collectonly",
-        "--collect-only",
-        action="store_true",
-        help="only collect tests, don't execute them.",
-    ),
+    group._addoption(
+        "-C",
+        "--collectonly",
+        "--collect-only",
+        action="store_true",
+        help="only collect tests, don't execute them.",
+    ),

Why this is sufficient:
- Adding "-C" registers a short option which argparse will parse and set the same destination config.option.collectonly (dest continues to be derived from the first long option; we preserve "--collectonly" as the first long option so dest remains "collectonly").
- The runtime code checks session.config.option.collectonly; no other code paths require changes.
- Help text will automatically include "-C, --collect-only" in the formatted help.
- No other global state or hardcoded values depend on the absence of a short form.

Now go through the required questions:

4a) "What assumption does my fix change?"
- Current assumption: the --collect-only option has only long-form names and no one-character shortcut; the CLI help and parsing code do not expect a short alias for collect-only. The fix removes that assumption by providing a short alias "-C".

4b) "What hardcoded values encode this assumption?"
- I reviewed the files related to option registration (src/_pytest/main.py and argument parsing machinery in src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py).
- Relevant hardcoded values:
  - In src/_pytest/main.py the current addoption call lists only the long options "--collectonly" and "--collect-only". This is the explicit encoding of "no short option". By changing that to include "-C", we remove that hardcoded omission.
  - In Argument.dest logic (argparsing.py), dest is derived from the first long option string: self.dest = self._long_opts[0][2:].replace("-", "_"). That code encodes the assumption that the first long option defines dest. This remains correct after adding the short option because we keep "--collectonly" (no hyphen) as the first long option, so dest remains "collectonly". No change needed.
  - No other hardcoded constants (numbers, offsets) relate to this option.

Conclusion for 4b: The only hardcoded value to change is the literal option registration itself (add "-C" to the optnames). The parsing/destination code does not need to change because it will still produce config.option.collectonly.

4c) "What about the reverse operation?"
- The reverse operation is parsing vs. using the parsed option later. Adding a short option only changes parsing; using the parsed value remains the same (config.option.collectonly is read unchanged).
- I re-read the parent's method list in nodes.FSCollector/Node/Collector (Checkpoint 3b). The complementary operations that could be affected by parsing are help text generation and any pre-parsing that checks for special args.
  - Parsing: argparse/MyOptionParser will accept "-C" and set the appropriate attribute in the namespace. No special preparse logic has special-cased collect-only; only "-p" and maybe "-h"/"--help"/"--version" have special handling in config._prepareconfig / preparse. I inspected config.__init__ and PytestPluginManager.consider_preparse which deals with "-p" and plugin-loading flags; no special-case code references collect-only.
  - Help generation: DropShorterLongHelpFormatter will format the help with the short option; tests that assert on exact help formatting might be affected. I searched tests and did not find tests asserting exact collect-only help output. Most tests use the long option in runpytest calls.
- Concrete substitution trace (parsing -> usage):
  - Variable: namespace attribute name for the option = dest = "collectonly"
  - Before change: if user typed "--collect-only", argparse sets namespace.collectonly = True -> config.option.collectonly True -> runtime uses this to skip running tests.
  - After change: if user types "-C", argparse sets namespace.collectonly = True -> config.option.collectonly True -> runtime uses same code path.
  - Conclusion: the complementary reading of the option is correct; no additional override of any parent method is necessary.

4d) "Does every existing method still work?"
- I reviewed methods in Session that reference config.option.collectonly and other code that performs collection and execution. The only relevant code is in pytest_runtestloop (main.py lines ~251-260):
  - It checks session.testsfailed and continue_on_collection_errors, then:
    if session.config.option.collectonly:
        return True
  - This remains correct because the dest is unchanged and argparse will set it when "-C" is used.
- Methods that produce help or parse options (argparsing/OptionGroup) continue to function since they already support both long and short options. No further method needs changes.

4e) "Does any NEW method need to be added?"
- I checked the parent class method list (Node, Collector) and all relevant methods. Adding a short option only affects parsing; no new runtime behavior is added or removed that would require new methods.
- Therefore NO new methods are required.
- One caveat: If any tests assume that the --collect-only help output has a specific format (e.g., no "-C" present), they could break. I searched for tests referencing --collect-only in help output (none found that check help exact text). If such a test exists externally, update the test to accept '-C' in help or update expected help text.
- No override of parent methods needed.

Checkpoint 5: Final report — root cause, locations to change, and complete fix

Root cause
- There is no single-character alias for the option --collect-only because the option is registered using only its long forms (--collectonly and --collect-only) in src/_pytest/main.py.
- The system made no assumption elsewhere that a short alias could or couldn't be present; it's simply not registered.

Every location that needs to change (complete fix)
1) Primary change: src/_pytest/main.py (pytest_addoption)
   - Current code:
     group.addoption(
         "--collectonly",
         "--collect-only",
         action="store_true",
         help="only collect tests, don't execute them.",
     ),
   - Problem / assumption encoded: option has only long forms; no short alias.
   - Fix: add a one-character short alias, e.g. "-C".
   - Replace with:
     group._addoption(
         "-C",
         "--collectonly",
         "--collect-only",
         action="store_true",
         help="only collect tests, don't execute them.",
     ),

   - Rationale: adding "-C" registers a short alias. Using _addoption aligns with other places registering single-character options. The dest remains "collectonly" (derived from the first long option), so runtime code that reads config.option.collectonly continues to work.

2) (No other code changes required in the core codebase.)
   - I inspected the parsing machinery in src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py and the plugin manager preparse logic in src/_pytest/config/__init__.py and found nothing that special-cases collect-only. The dest and runtime reading logic remains correct.
   - I searched the test suite for exact help formatting that might break; tests use --collect-only in runpytest invocations and do not appear to assert on exact help text for this option. If any external test asserts exact help output, update that test accordingly.

Hardcoded values that need to become dynamic
- None. The only literal to change is the option registration itself (the optnames array). No numeric constants, offsets, or parsing assumptions encode "no short option".

Any methods that need to be added or overridden
- None. No parent methods need to be overridden; parsing sets the same dest and runtime reads it via config.option.collectonly.

Notes and follow-up
- Chosen short alias: "-C" (uppercase C). This minimizes collision with existing lowercase single-letter options that pytest already uses widely.
- Alternative choices:
  - A lowercase letter could be chosen, but many lowercase letters are already used; picking uppercase avoids collisions and respects the convention in addoption that lowercase single-letter flags are reserved and usually set via _addoption.
- Tests:
  - Run the full test suite to confirm no tests rely on exact help formatting or presence/absence of single-letter flags for collect-only.
  - If any test asserts exact help output including options ordering, update those expectations (help output may change to include "-C, --collect-only").
- Documentation:
  - Update documentation and examples if you want to advertise the new shortcut in docs or release notes.

Summary / TL;DR
- Minimal safe fix: add "-C" as a short option for --collect-only at the option registration in src/_pytest/main.py:
  change group.addoption("--collectonly","--collect-only", ...) to group._addoption("-C","--collectonly","--collect-only", ...).
- This sets config.option.collectonly when "-C" is used and no other code needs changes. Run tests to verify no unexpected breakages (particularly help formatting tests).

===HIGH_LEVEL===

- Bug: --collect-only has no one-character shortcut; the option is registered only with long names (no short alias). This is encoded in src/_pytest/main.py (pytest_addoption). (Checkpoint 1)
- Root cause: option registration omitted a short optstring. Runtime reads config.option.collectonly (pytest_runtestloop) so adding a short alias only needs to change registration; no runtime API change is required. (Checkpoint 1,2)
- Fix strategy: add a short optstring to the existing registration in src/_pytest/main.py. Use group._addoption with "-C" plus the existing long names so dest remains collectonly. This covers all code paths that check config.option.collectonly. (Checkpoint 4)
- Hardcoded values to change: the literal option names in pytest_addoption in src/_pytest/main.py — add "-C" (no other numeric/constants need changing). Preserve the first long opt so dest formula continues to produce "collectonly" (dest = first_long[2:].replace("-", "_")). (Checkpoint 2)
- Methods to add/override: none. Parsing produces the same dest; runtime code (pytest_runtestloop and other readers of config.option.collectonly) continue to work. (Checkpoint 4)
- Investigation gaps: I did not (a) scan every plugin/third-party extension for an existing "-C" use, (b) run tests to detect help-output or documentation breakages, nor (c) search for tests that assert exact help formatting that might need updates. These should be checked before merging. (Checkpoint 5)

===MID_LEVEL===

### Finding 1: Option registration lacks a short alias (root cause)
What: The CLI option --collect-only is registered with only long forms in pytest_addoption, so no single-letter shortcut exists.  
Where: src/_pytest/main.py :: pytest_addoption (registration uses group.addoption("--collectonly", "--collect-only", action="store_true", ...)).  
Why it matters: This is the single place to change to add a shortcut; runtime code reads config.option.collectonly and will accept any alias that sets that same dest. (Checkpoint 1)

### Finding 2: Runtime reads the same dest — no runtime change needed
What: Code that disables running tests when collecting uses the same option destination.  
Where: src/_pytest/main.py :: pytest_runtestloop — contains: if session.config.option.collectonly: return True  
Why it matters: Because dest is "collectonly", adding a short alias that maps to that dest is sufficient; no other runtime paths need modification. (Checkpoint 1)

### Finding 3: Parser dest derivation must be preserved
What: Argument dest is derived from the first long option; formula: dest = self._long_opts[0][2:].replace("-", "_").  
Where: src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py (Argument/addoption logic).  
Why it matters: Keep "--collectonly" (or equivalent) as a long option so dest remains "collectonly"; otherwise code that reads config.option.collectonly will break. (Checkpoint 2)

### Finding 4: Short-option registration rules and safe choice
What: OptionGroup.addoption/_addoption differ: lowercase single-letter shortopts are restricted unless using _addoption (shortupper behavior); many lowercase letters are already used. Uppercase "-C" is unused in core and avoids colliding with existing lowercase short flags.  
Where: src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py :: OptionGroup.addoption/_addoption (enforces "lowercase shortoptions reserved" when shortupper False).  
Why it matters: Choosing "-C" and registering via _addoption (or addoption if uppercase allowed) keeps behavior consistent and avoids collisions. (Checkpoint 2,3)

### Finding 5: Tests/docs/help could be impacted (gap)
What: Help output will now show "-C, --collect-only"; tests or docs that check exact help output or examples may break. I did not run tests or locate such assertions.  
Where to check: tests under testing/* that reference help output, and docs in doc/en and CHANGELOG references to --collect-only examples.  
Why it matters: A complete fix requires updating any tests/docs that assert exact help text or examples. (Checkpoint 5)

===LOW_LEVEL===

### Location: src/_pytest/main.py:pytest_addoption
- **Wrong assumption**: The code assumes collect-only only needs long-form names (no one-character shortcut).
- **Evidence**: registration observed as:
  group.addoption(
      "--collectonly",
      "--collect-only",
      action="store_true",
      help="only collect tests, don't execute them.",
  ),
  (search found this in src/_pytest/main.py; this is the explicit place to add a short alias). (Checkpoint 1)
- **Related locations**: src/_pytest/main.py::pytest_runtestloop (reads config.option.collectonly); src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py (dest derivation).

### Location: src/_pytest/main.py:pytest_runtestloop
- **Wrong assumption**: None in need of code change here — but this is the sink that validates the fix.
- **Evidence**: runtime check is:
  if session.config.option.collectonly:
      return True
  (so any alias that sets dest "collectonly" will trigger the behavior). (Checkpoint 1)
- **Related locations**: src/_pytest/main.py::pytest_addoption (must preserve dest naming).

### Location: src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py:addoption (dest derivation)
- **Wrong assumption**: The parser derives the option attribute name (dest) from the first long option; changing order of optnames would change dest.
- **Evidence / formula**: dest is derived from the first long option using the formula:
  dest = self._long_opts[0][2:].replace("-", "_")
  (i.e. strip leading "--" from the first long option then replace hyphens with underscores). Keep "--collectonly" or equivalent first to preserve dest "collectonly". (Checkpoint 2)
- **Related locations**: src/_pytest/main.py::pytest_addoption (must keep first long name consistent), any code reading config.option.collectonly.

### Location: src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py:OptionGroup._addoption / addoption
- **Wrong assumption**: addoption forbids lowercase one-letter shortopts (they're "reserved") unless special internal flag is used; code using addoption vs _addoption determines whether lowercase single-letter opts are allowed.
- **Evidence**: OptionGroup._addoption exists to allow shortupper=True and avoid raising ValueError("lowercase shortoptions reserved") for lowercase shortopts. (Checkpoint 2)
- **Related locations**: src/_pytest/main.py (existing single-letter flags use group._addoption for lowercase flags like "-x", "-s", "-k" — so if a lowercase letter was chosen it should be registered via _addoption).

### Location: tests & docs (not a code file; action item)
- **Wrong assumption**: No tests or docs will fail because of adding a short alias.
- **Evidence**: I found many test invocations that use "--collect-only" (testing/*) but did not find or run any tests asserting exact help text; I did not search comprehensively for help-output assertions. This is an uncovered risk. (Checkpoint 5)
- **Related locations**: doc/en examples, testing/*, CHANGELOG.rst references to --collectonly.

Final recommended change (implementation note)
- Replace the addoption call in src/_pytest/main.py with a registration that includes "-C", for example:
  group._addoption(
      "-C",
      "--collectonly",
      "--collect-only",
      action="store_true",
      help="only collect tests, don't execute them.",
  ),
- Rationale: keeps dest "collectonly" (first long option unchanged), registers an unused uppercase short "-C", avoids lowercase-letter collisions, and requires no other code changes. Run full test suite and scan docs/tests for help-output expectations before merging. (Checkpoint 4,5)

Thought: Phase 1 — Verification and completeness check

I will verify each diagnostic claim in the draft against the actual source code and the investigation analysis, and note missing or unsupported claims.

1) Verify pytest_addoption registration (claim: collect-only registered only with long names)
- Why I checked: the draft asserts the collect-only option is registered without any short alias in pytest_addoption.
- What I looked up: src/_pytest/main.py around the option group for "collect".
- Evidence found:
  - src/_pytest/main.py lines 108-114 (exact excerpt):
    group.addoption(
        "--collectonly",
        "--collect-only",
        action="store_true",
        help="only collect tests, don't execute them.",
    ),
  - This shows the option is registered with two long forms only (no short "-X" form).
- Conclusion: Claim verified — the option is registered only with long-form names in pytest_addoption.

2) Verify runtime readers use the same dest (claim: runtime reads config.option.collectonly)
- Why I checked: the draft claims there is no runtime change needed because code reads the dest "collectonly".
- What I looked up: src/_pytest/main.py pytest_runtestloop and other places that check the option.
- Evidence found:
  - src/_pytest/main.py lines 251-259:
    if session.config.option.collectonly:
        return True
  - src/_pytest/logging.py lines 705-710:
    @pytest.hookimpl(hookwrapper=True)
    def pytest_runtestloop(self, session):
        if session.config.option.collectonly:
            yield
            return
  - src/_pytest/terminal.py lines 619-627 and 627-633 show terminal code checking config.getoption("collectonly") to decide whether to print collected items and collection failures.
  - src/_pytest/pytester.py lines 811-819 show inline_genitems runs "--collect-only".
- Conclusion: Claim verified — multiple runtime/printing hooks rely on the same dest (collectonly). Any alias that maps to that dest will be visible to these code paths.

3) Verify dest derivation (claim: argparsing derives dest from the first long option)
- Why I checked: changing option names or their order can alter config.option attribute names at runtime; the draft states the formula.
- What I looked up: src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py Argument class.
- Evidence found:
  - src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py lines 223-231:
      self._set_opt_strings(names)
      if not self.dest:
          if self._long_opts:
              self.dest = self._long_opts[0][2:].replace("-", "_")
          else:
              try:
                  self.dest = self._short_opts[0][1:]
- Conclusion: Claim verified and the formula shown in the draft (strip leading "--" from the first long option, replace "-" with "_") is accurate.

4) Verify short-option registration constraints (claim: lowercase 1-letter shortopts are reserved by addoption/_addoption rules)
- Why I checked: the draft asserts that OptionGroup.addoption vs _addoption implement rules that forbid registering lowercase single-letter shortopts unless a special path is used.
- What I looked up: src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py OptionGroup.addoption/_addoption/_addoption_instance.
- Evidence found:
  - src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py lines 312-321 and 322-327:
      option = Argument(*optnames, **attrs)
      self._addoption_instance(option, shortupper=False)
    def _addoption(self, *optnames, **attrs):
      option = Argument(*optnames, **attrs)
      self._addoption_instance(option, shortupper=True)
    def _addoption_instance(self, option, shortupper=False):
      if not shortupper:
          for opt in option._short_opts:
              if opt[0] == "-" and opt[1].islower():
                  raise ValueError("lowercase shortoptions reserved")
- Conclusion: Claim verified — addoption (shortupper=False) raises for lowercase short single-letter options; _addoption allows them.

5) Check for collisions of "-C" in the codebase
- Why I checked: the draft recommends "-C" as a safe uppercase choice and asserts "-C" is unused.
- What I searched: repository for literal "-C".
- Evidence found:
  - search for '"-C"' (in *.py) returned No matches.
- Conclusion: No core usages of a "-C" short option were found in the scanned Python files (no direct collision in core). This does not guarantee plugin or third-party absence.

6) Tests/docs references to "--collect-only" (claim: tests/docs may reference exact help output or examples)
- Why I checked: the draft flags tests/docs as a risk.
- What I looked up: search for "collectonly" and examples from tests/docs.
- Evidence found:
  - search results show many references, including:
    - doc/en/example/pythoncollection.py (example run: "# run this with $ pytest --collect-only test_collectonly.py")
    - testing/test_terminal.py has an entire TestCollectonly class that invokes pytest with "--collect-only" in many tests (e.g. lines 286, 298, 310, 315, 325, 339, 371, 377, 382).
    - src/_pytest/pytester.py inline_genitems uses "--collect-only".
    - src/_pytest/logging.py and src/_pytest/terminal.py both check config option getoption("collectonly") as noted above.
- Conclusion: The draft's caution is warranted — there are many tests and docs that reference the literal option text. The investigation did not exhaustively search for tests asserting exact help output formatting; such occurrences remain a real risk.

7) Check for any unsupported or inaccurate claims in the draft
- The draft's numbered root-cause points are generally supported by code.
- One subtlety: the draft says "OptionGroup.addoption/_addoption enforces rules that forbid registering lowercase single-letter shortopts via the normal addoption path ... so adding a short alias requires using the appropriate registration path or choosing an uppercase short option." That is accurate.
- The draft's MID_LEVEL assertion "many lowercase letters are already used" is plausible but not formally verified in the draft report; I did not enumerate all single-letter short options, but the codebase clearly uses several single-letter flags (e.g. "-x", "-c", "-k", "-s" etc.). This is a minor, non-critical phrasing; I will avoid asserting a count.
- The draft included a "Final recommended change (implementation note)" that prescribes a fix (add "-C" using group._addoption). Per the report rules, the report must be a diagnosis only, not a prescription. I will remove that recommendation from the final report content and keep only the diagnostic information.

8) Completeness check — ensure all secondary findings with the same root-cause pattern are included
- The investigation analysis identified multiple locations that share the same root cause (i.e., the option is only registered under long names and runtime code reads the dest). The draft RootCause listed pytest_addoption and argparsing behavior and pytest_runtestloop; however:
  - It did not explicitly list other code that reads the same dest (src/_pytest/logging.py and src/_pytest/terminal.py). Those are secondary findings that share the same root cause (they rely on config.option.collectonly), so they must be explicitly listed in RootCause with equal weight.
  - It did mention tests/docs impact, but did not enumerate the concrete files (pytester.py, testing/test_terminal.py, doc/en/example). I will add these specific locations to RootCause so every occurrence found in the investigation is represented.
- I also checked that adding a short alias (if done) would not require changing runtime readers because they read config.option.collectonly — this supports the "no runtime change needed" observation in the draft.

Summary of what I will change in the draft:
- Keep Title, Description, StepsToReproduce, ExpectedBehavior, ObservedBehavior largely as-is (concise, non-prescriptive).
- Rewrite RootCause to:
  - Preserve the same findings but rephrase to be strictly diagnostic (no fix recommendations).
  - Add the additional locations that rely on the same dest (src/_pytest/logging.py, src/_pytest/terminal.py, src/_pytest/pytester.py, testing/* and a doc example).
  - Keep the dest-derivation formula exactly as evidence.
  - Remove the final "implementation note" that prescribes a concrete modification.

- Produce an "evidence" list with file:line snippets and references supporting each RootCause point.

bug_report.Title: pytest --collect-only has no one-character shortcut
bug_report.Description: The --collect-only CLI option is registered only with long-form names and has no single-character alias; users must type the full long option and help output shows no one-character shortcut.
bug_report.RootCause:
1) Missing short-option registration in option declaration: src/_pytest/main.py::pytest_addoption registers the collect-only option only with long names ("--collectonly", "--collect-only"); no short optstring is provided, which is why no one-character alias exists. (See: src/_pytest/main.py addoption call for collect-only.)

2) Option destination name depends on first long option: src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py::Argument derives the option destination (the attribute available at runtime as config.option.<dest>) using the formula dest = self._long_opts[0][2:].replace("-", "_"). Because the dest is computed from the first long option, changing the first long option or its order will change the runtime attribute name (currently "collectonly").

3) Short-option registration constraints enforced by parser/group code: src/_pytest/config/argparsing.py::OptionGroup._addoption_instance enforces that when shortupper is False (the normal addoption path), lowercase single-letter short options raise a ValueError("lowercase shortoptions reserved"). This means lowercase single-letter shortopts cannot be registered through the usual addoption API without using the special _addoption path or otherwise circumventing that check; uppercase single-letter shortopts are not rejected by that check.

4) Multiple runtime and reporting locations read the same dest: several runtime and reporting code paths read config.option.collectonly (the dest produced from the first long option). Notable locations observed in the codebase that rely on that dest are:
   - src/_pytest/main.py::pytest_runtestloop (if session.config.option.collectonly: return True)
   - src/_pytest/logging.py::pytest_runtestloop hook (checks session.config.option.collectonly)
   - src/_pytest/terminal.py (uses config.getoption("collectonly") to control printing of collected items and collection failures)
   - src/_pytest/pytester.py (test helper inline_genitems invokes the literal "--collect-only")
   Any alias that maps to the same dest will affect all these locations uniformly.

5) Tests and documentation reference the literal long option: many test files and doc examples invoke or mention "--collect-only" explicitly (for example testing/test_terminal.py::TestCollectonly, src/_pytest/pytester.py::inline_genitems, doc/en/example/pythoncollection.py). The investigation did not exhaustively search for tests or docs that assert exact help output formatting; such assertions (if present) would be affected by any change to help text or option presentation.

Each of the above is an equal-weight finding: the problem is that the option was declared without a short optstring in the single authoritative registration location (src/_pytest/main.py::pytest_addoption), the parser derives the runtime attribute name from the first long-form option, and the option-group/parser code enforces constraints on which single-letter short options can be added via the standard API. Multiple runtime/reporting locations and tests/docs depend on the existing dest/option name.
StepsToReproduce: Run `pytest -h` (help) and inspect the entry for collect-only; observe only the long option forms are shown (no single-character alias).
Attempt to invoke pytest with a one-character short flag for collect-only (e.g. `pytest -C`) and observe it is not accepted as an alias for --collect-only.
bug_report.ExpectedBehavior: Help output shows a one-character alias for the collect-only option (for example: `-C, --collect-only`) and invoking that single-character alias behaves identically to `--collect-only` (pytest only collects tests and does not execute them).
bug_report.ObservedBehavior: No one-character alias is available for the collect-only option; help shows only `--collectonly`/`--collect-only`, and users must use the full long option to trigger collection-only behavior.

method_cache.src/_pytest/capture.py:
class NoCapture:
    EMPTY_BUFFER = None
    __init__ = start = done = suspend = resume = lambda *args: None


class FDCaptureBinary:
    """Capture IO to/from a given os-level filedescriptor.

    snap() produces `bytes`
    """

    EMPTY_BUFFER = b""
    _state = None

    def __init__(self, targetfd, tmpfile=None):
        self.targetfd = targetfd
        try:
            self.targetfd_save = os.dup(self.targetfd)
        except OSError:
            self.start = lambda: None
            self.done = lambda: None
        else:
            self.start = self._start
            self.done = self._done
            if targetfd == 0:
                assert not tmpfile, "cannot set tmpfile with stdin"
                tmpfile = open(os.devnull)
                self.syscapture = SysCapture(targetfd)
            else:
                if tmpfile is None:
                    tmpfile = EncodedFile(
                        TemporaryFile(buffering=0),
                        encoding="utf-8",
                        errors="replace",
                        write_through=True,
                    )
                if targetfd in patchsysdict:
                    self.syscapture = SysCapture(targetfd, tmpfile)
                else:
                    self.syscapture = NoCapture()
            self.tmpfile = tmpfile
            self.tmpfile_fd = tmpfile.fileno()

    def __repr__(self):
        return "<{} {} oldfd={} _state={!r} tmpfile={}>".format(
            self.__class__.__name__,
            self.targetfd,
            getattr(self, "targetfd_save", "<UNSET>"),
            self._state,
            hasattr(self, "tmpfile") and repr(self.tmpfile) or "<UNSET>",
        )

    def _start(self):
        """ Start capturing on targetfd using memorized tmpfile. """
        try:
            os.fstat(self.targetfd_save)
        except (AttributeError, OSError):
            raise ValueError("saved filedescriptor not valid anymore")
        os.dup2(self.tmpfile_fd, self.targetfd)
        self.syscapture.start()
        self._state = "started"

    def snap(self):
        self.tmpfile.seek(0)
        res = self.tmpfile.buffer.read()
        self.tmpfile.seek(0)
        self.tmpfile.truncate()
        return res

    def _done(self):
        """ stop capturing, restore streams, return original capture file,
        seeked to position zero. """
        targetfd_save = self.__dict__.pop("targetfd_save")
        os.dup2(targetfd_save, self.targetfd)
        os.close(targetfd_save)
        self.syscapture.done()
        self.tmpfile.close()
        self._state = "done"

    def suspend(self):
        self.syscapture.suspend()
        os.dup2(self.targetfd_save, self.targetfd)
        self._state = "suspended"

    def resume(self):
        self.syscapture.resume()
        os.dup2(self.tmpfile_fd, self.targetfd)
        self._state = "resumed"

    def writeorg(self, data):
        """ write to original file descriptor. """
        os.write(self.targetfd_save, data)


class FDCapture(FDCaptureBinary):
    """Capture IO to/from a given os-level filedescriptor.

    snap() produces text
    """

    # Ignore type because it doesn't match the type in the superclass (bytes).
    EMPTY_BUFFER = ""  # type: ignore

    def snap(self):
        self.tmpfile.seek(0)
        res = self.tmpfile.read()
        self.tmpfile.seek(0)
        self.tmpfile.truncate()
        return res

    def writeorg(self, data):
        """ write to original file descriptor. """
        data = data.encode("utf-8")  # XXX use encoding of original stream
        os.write(self.targetfd_save, data)


class SysCaptureBinary:

    EMPTY_BUFFER = b""
    _state = None

    def __init__(self, fd, tmpfile=None):
        name = patchsysdict[fd]
        self._old = getattr(sys, name)
        self.name = name
        if tmpfile is None:
            if name == "stdin":
                tmpfile = DontReadFromInput()
            else:
                tmpfile = CaptureIO()
        self.tmpfile = tmpfile

    def __repr__(self):
        return "<{} {} _old={} _state={!r} tmpfile={!r}>".format(
            self.__class__.__name__,
            self.name,
            hasattr(self, "_old") and repr(self._old) or "<UNSET>",
            self._state,
            self.tmpfile,
        )

    def start(self):
        setattr(sys, self.name, self.tmpfile)
        self._state = "started"

    def snap(self):
        res = self.tmpfile.buffer.getvalue()
        self.tmpfile.seek(0)
        self.tmpfile.truncate()
        return res

    def done(self):
        setattr(sys, self.name, self._old)
        del self._old
        self.tmpfile.close()
        self._state = "done"

    def suspend(self):
        setattr(sys, self.name, self._old)
        self._state = "suspended"

    def resume(self):
        setattr(sys, self.name, self.tmpfile)
        self._state = "resumed"

    def writeorg(self, data):
        self._old.flush()
        self._old.buffer.write(data)
        self._old.buffer.flush()


class SysCapture(SysCaptureBinary):
    EMPTY_BUFFER = ""  # type: ignore[assignment]  # noqa: F821

    def snap(self):
        res = self.tmpfile.getvalue()
        self.tmpfile.seek(0)
        self.tmpfile.truncate()
        return res

    def writeorg(self, data):
        self._old.write(data)
        self._old.flush()


class TeeSysCapture(SysCapture):
    def __init__(self, fd, tmpfile=None):
        name = patchsysdict[fd]
        self._old = getattr(sys, name)
        self.name = name
        if tmpfile is None:
            if name == "stdin":
                tmpfile = DontReadFromInput()
            else:
                tmpfile = TeeCaptureIO(self._old)
        self.tmpfile = tmpfile

Thought: Checkpoint 1: Identify the bug

1a) classify_report findings (from the report)
- Key classes / methods mentioned: SomeClass (user), SafeRepr / saferepr (pytest), _format_repr_exception, _try_repr_or_str, repr_instance, repr, ExceptionInfo, repr_traceback, repr_args, repr_locals.
- The traceback shows the failure occurs while pytest is trying to build a safe representation of an object: stack goes into _pytest/_io/saferepr.py::_format_repr_exception and eventually into the user's __repr__ / __getattribute__.

Reason for next tool call: I need to inspect pytest's saferepr implementation to see how it calls into user code and identify the exact lines responsible.

1b) search_codebase results
- Found the file src/_pytest/_io/saferepr.py (SafeRepr and helper functions).
- Found src/_pytest/_code/code.py which uses saferepr extensively when formatting tracebacks / exception info.

1c) I opened the two relevant source files:
- src/_pytest/_io/saferepr.py (entire file)
- src/_pytest/_code/code.py (key parts where saferepr is used)

What I read (high level):
- saferepr.py defines:
  - _try_repr_or_str(obj): tries repr(obj), on any BaseException returns '{}("{}")'.format(type(obj).__name__, obj)
  - _format_repr_exception(exc, obj): tries to render a representation for exc (via _try_repr_or_str), then returns "<[{} raised in repr()] {} object at 0x{:x}>".format(exc_info, obj.__class__.__name__, id(obj))
  - class SafeRepr(reprlib.Repr): overrides repr() and repr_instance() to catch exceptions during repr and use _format_repr_exception
- code.py uses saferepr in many places when formatting exceptions/traces (ExceptionInfo.__repr__, FormattedExcinfo.repr_locals, etc).

1d) Clear statement of the bug and primary responsible method/class
- Observed bug: Pytest gets an INTERNALERROR when trying to format an exception if the object whose repr is being printed has broken __getattribute__ or __repr__ that raise (especially if they use "raise" without an active exception). The immediate error in the session is "RuntimeError: No active exception to reraise" coming from user code, but it is raised while pytest is trying to format the object for error reporting, causing an internal error during exception formatting.
- Root cause location (primary): src/_pytest/_io/saferepr.py::_format_repr_exception and its helper _try_repr_or_str. In particular:
  - _format_repr_exception accesses obj.__class__.__name__ (which triggers attribute lookup on the object and thus can call user-defined __getattribute__).
  - _try_repr_or_str fallback formatting calls format(..., obj) which invokes str(obj) / __format__ and can call user code as well.

Both cause re-entrancy into user code while building the fallback for a failing repr, leading to additional exceptions (and ultimately an internal error while pytest itself was trying to report a user exception).

  - Note: the fallback uses the object (obj) directly — which calls obj.__str__/__format__ and therefore can execute user code.
- _format_repr_exception(exc, obj)
  - tries exc_info = _try_repr_or_str(exc); on failure uses exc_info = "unpresentable exception ({})".format(_try_repr_or_str(exc))
  - returns "<[{} raised in repr()] {} object at 0x{:x}>".format(exc_info, obj.__class__.__name__, id(obj))
  - Note: uses obj.__class__.__name__ which performs attribute access on obj; that can call its __getattribute__ (user code).
- _ellipsize(s, maxsize)
- safeformat(obj): uses pprint.pformat(obj) with fallback to _format_repr_exception
- saferepr(obj, maxsize=240): wrapper returning SafeRepr(maxsize).repr(obj)
- _pformat_dispatch and AlwaysDispatchingPrettyPrinter described above
